title: Tesla Q1 2026: Earnings Growth Amid Rising AI Investment Costs
text: Tesla reported strong quarterly revenue growth of 16% to $22.4 billion, but mounting AI development expenses strained profitability. Net income grew modestly at 17% to $477 million while operating expenses surged 37% to $3.78 billion, contracting operating margin to 4.2%. CEO Elon Musk has committed substantial resources toward autonomous vehicles, humanoid robots, and semiconductor manufacturing. On earnings call, Musk acknowledged planning very significant capital expenditure increase, characterizing investments as justified by future revenue potential. Tesla reallocates manufacturing capacity away from vehicles: discontinued Model S sedan and Model X crossover lines. EV deliveries totaled 358,023 units, up 6% annually but below consensus estimates of 370,000 units.
